title: thymophylla mutica
intro: Thymophylla mutica, also known as the hairy leaf or sticky groundsel, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). Thymophylla mutica has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its high content of terpenes and essential oils. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, burns, and skin conditions, due to its antibacterial and antifungal properties, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of infection. In herbal preparations, thymophylla mutica is often used in tinctures, salves, and infusions to take advantage of its medicinal properties, making it a popular choice among herbalists and natural health practitioners.
